questions.q: On the banks of which river does the city of Agra stand?
questions.explanation: Agra lies on the banks of the Yamuna river in present-day Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: Agra is best known as a principal capital of which empire?
questions.explanation: Agra served as a principal capital of the Mughal Empire, especially under Akbar and Shah Jahan.

questions.q: Which famous white marble mausoleum stands in Agra?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal, built by Shah Jahan, is located in Agra on the Yamuna.

questions.q: Which great fort, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, stands in Agra?
questions.explanation: The Agra Fort is one of the empire's most celebrated monuments and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is Agra located?
questions.explanation: Agra is a city in the modern Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: The Taj Mahal in Agra was built by Shah Jahan in memory of whom?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan built the Taj Mahal as a mausoleum for his wife Mumtaz Mahal.

questions.q: Two of the Mughal Empire's most celebrated monuments in Agra are the Taj Mahal and the:
questions.explanation: Agra is home to both the Agra Fort and the Taj Mahal, two of the empire's most celebrated monuments.

questions.q: Of what material is the Taj Mahal at Agra principally built?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal is a white marble mausoleum, a masterpiece of Mughal architecture.

questions.q: Agra rose to its greatest prominence under the rule of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Though earlier a Lodi seat, Agra flourished as a Mughal capital under Akbar and Shah Jahan.

questions.q: Which two Mughal emperors are most associated with making Agra a capital?
questions.explanation: Agra served as a principal Mughal capital especially under Akbar and Shah Jahan.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor largely rebuilt the Agra Fort in red sandstone beginning in 1565?
questions.explanation: Akbar rebuilt the Agra Fort in red sandstone from 1565, replacing the older brick fortress.

questions.q: Before the Mughals, Agra served as a capital under which sultan of the Delhi Sultanate?
questions.explanation: Sikandar Lodi shifted the Lodi capital to Agra around 1504, before the Mughal conquest.

questions.q: In which Agra-area palace did Shah Jahan spend his final years confined by his son?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb imprisoned his father Shah Jahan in the Agra Fort, where he died in 1666.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor's tomb is located at Sikandra, just outside Agra?
questions.explanation: Akbar's tomb stands at Sikandra near Agra, a notable example of Mughal funerary architecture.

questions.q: The 'Baby Taj', an early marble tomb in Agra, was built for which noble, father of Nur Jahan?
questions.explanation: The tomb of Itimad-ud-Daulah, Nur Jahan's father, is often called the 'Baby Taj' in Agra.

questions.q: Which Mughal capital did Akbar build a short distance from Agra in the 1570s?
questions.explanation: Akbar built Fatehpur Sikri near Agra as his capital in the 1570s before it was abandoned.

questions.q: The Taj Mahal at Agra was completed around which year?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal was completed around 1653 during the reign of Shah Jahan.

questions.q: Which emperor moved the Mughal capital from Agra to a new city at Delhi in the 17th century?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan shifted the capital from Agra to his new city of Shahjahanabad in Delhi.

questions.q: Which famous founder of the Mughal dynasty died at Agra in 1530?
questions.explanation: Babur, founder of the Mughal Empire, died at Agra in 1530.

questions.q: Within the Agra Fort, which white marble mosque was built by Shah Jahan?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan built the Moti Masjid, or Pearl Mosque, within the Agra Fort.

questions.q: Which Persian-style charbagh garden, possibly the earliest Mughal garden, was laid out by Babur across the Yamuna at Agra?
questions.explanation: Babur laid out the Ram Bagh, or Aram Bagh, at Agra, often called the earliest Mughal garden in India.

questions.q: The Agra Fort and the Taj Mahal are both recognised internationally with what status?
questions.explanation: Both the Agra Fort and the Taj Mahal are inscribed as UNESCO World Heritage Sites.

questions.q: Which Persian architect is traditionally credited with the chief design of the Taj Mahal at Agra?
questions.explanation: Ustad Ahmad Lahauri is traditionally credited as the principal architect of the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: From which marble within the Agra Fort did Shah Jahan reportedly gaze toward the Taj Mahal during his captivity?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan is said to have viewed the Taj Mahal from the Musamman Burj, an octagonal tower in the Agra Fort.

questions.q: What was the older name by which the Agra region was known in some early medieval references?
questions.explanation: Agra was anciently associated with the name Agravana (Agrabana), meaning a grove or forest at the frontier.

questions.q: Which gemstone-inlay technique decorating Agra's Taj Mahal involves setting semi-precious stones into marble?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal's marble is decorated with pietra dura, known in India as parchin kari inlay work.

questions.q: The Lodi sultan who first established Agra as a capital did so around which year?
questions.explanation: Sikandar Lodi founded Agra as a Lodi capital around 1504, before the Mughal era.

questions.q: Which Jat ruler of Bharatpur sacked Agra and the Agra Fort in the 18th century amid Mughal decline?
questions.explanation: The Jat ruler Suraj Mal of Bharatpur attacked and plundered Agra in 1761 during the empire's decline.

questions.q: The Mughal capital frequently shifted between Agra and which other city during the dynasty's history?
questions.explanation: The Mughal seat of power alternated chiefly between Agra and Delhi over the centuries.

questions.q: Which English ambassador to Jahangir's court travelled to Agra and left an influential account of the Mughal court?
questions.explanation: Sir Thomas Roe, envoy of King James I, visited the Mughal court including Agra and recorded his observations.
